ingredients: 1/2 cucumber, seeded and cut into matchsticks
8 oz sushi-grade fish (e.g., salmon or tuna) or imitation crab
steps: Rinse rice until water runs mostly clear. Cook rice with water (rice cooker or covered pot). Rest 10 minutes, then transfer to a wide bowl.
Warm rice vinegar, sugar, and salt until dissolved. Fold into hot rice while fanning to cool to room temperature.
Prep fillings (slice fish; cut vegetables). Set up a bamboo mat lined with plastic wrap.
Place nori shiny-side down. With wet hands, spread a thin, even layer of rice over nori, leaving 1 inch at the far edge. Add a thin line of wasabi if using.
Arrange fillings across the near third. Roll tightly using the mat, pressing to form a firm cylinder. Moisten the far edge to seal.
Slice into 6 to 8 pieces with a very sharp, wet knife. Serve with soy sauce and pickled ginger.
tips: Use sushi-grade fish from a reputable seller and keep everything very cold; wet your hands and knife to prevent sticking.
